title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
summary: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) has added new capabilities for AI workloads including training, serving, reinforcement learning, and agentic workflows. The platform is designed to improve reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness for AI infrastructure that is shifting from frontier model serving to complex agentic systems.
writeup: This Google Cloud Tech video surveys recent additions to Google Kubernetes Engine for AI workloads, spanning training, model serving, reinforcement learning, and agentic workflows. It frames the changes around AI infrastructure shifting from frontier model serving toward complex agentic systems, with stated goals of better reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. The takeaway is that GKE now positions itself as a platform for enterprise AI across the full lifecycle, including test-time compute and reinforcement learning, not just model deployment.
